title: Dropdown
description: Dropdown handles single and multi-select choices from predefined options. It supports searchable lists, clear actions, compact trigger mode, async data sources, custom item templates, and shared field states for denser form and filter workflows.
intro: Dropdown is appropriate when users choose from a bounded set rather than entering freeform text. In addition to standard field variants and validation, it covers multi-select tags, compact filter triggers, server-backed search, and custom item presentation without forcing consumers to build the overlay behavior themselves.

featureSections.title: Basic single select
featureSections.description: Start with a stable option set, a clear label, and helpful placeholder or helper text only when they reduce ambiguity rather than repeat the label.

featureSections.title: Variants, sizes, and field states
featureSections.description: Dropdown inherits the shared field shell, so visual variants, density, required styling, readonly, and disabled states should look consistent with the rest of the form system.

featureSections.title: Multi-select with tags
featureSections.description: Use multi mode when the list represents a set of filters or recipients. Selected items stay visible as tags and can be removed without reopening the panel.

featureSections.title: Search, clear, and disabled options
featureSections.description: Searchable and clearable affordances matter most when the option set is long enough to justify them. Disabled items stay visible for context but are skipped by keyboard and selection logic.

featureSections.title: Compact trigger and panel sizing
featureSections.description: Compact mode turns the control into a lightweight toolbar or table filter trigger. Fixed or bounded panel widths keep dense filter UI predictable instead of stretching to arbitrary content.

featureSections.title: Async data source and paged search
featureSections.description: When the option set is large or remote, bind a data source so filtering and paging happen outside the component. Search then behaves like a query input instead of an in-memory filter.

featureSections.title: Custom item template
featureSections.description: Some dropdown choices need more than a single label. A projected item template can add secondary metadata or richer layout while the component still owns keyboard, focus, and selection behavior.

featureSections.title: Form composition
featureSections.description: Show dropdown as part of a real workflow, mixing single and multi-select fields with actions and a compact state summary so the example is reusable instead of purely illustrative.

accessibility.title: Accessible name and role
accessibility.body:
The standard trigger uses `ariaLabel` when provided and otherwise falls back to the visible `label`. Compact mode also needs an explicit accessible name because the trigger is just an icon button.

| Mode / input | Exposed semantics |
| --- | --- |
| `single` | `combobox` trigger controlling a `listbox` |
| `multi` | `listbox` trigger with `aria-multiselectable="true"` |
| `ariaLabel` | explicit accessible name override |
| visible `label` | default accessible name fallback |

accessibility.title: Keyboard model
accessibility.body:
Disabled items are skipped during navigation, and typeahead moves focus to the next matching option.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `ArrowDown` / `ArrowUp` | opens the panel and moves through selectable items |
| `Home` / `End` | jumps to the first or last selectable item |
| `PageUp` / `PageDown` | moves through longer lists in larger steps |
| `Enter` / `Space` | selects the active option |
| `Escape` | closes the panel and returns focus to the trigger |
| `Tab` | leaves the control and closes the panel |
| printable characters | runs typeahead search against option labels |

accessibility.title: Active descendant and selection state
accessibility.body:
The component tracks the active option through `aria-activedescendant`. In multi-select mode, the visible tags are only a sighted summary; the authoritative accessibility state remains on the trigger and list options.

| Input / state | Attribute |
| --- | --- |
| active option | `aria-activedescendant` |
| open state | `aria-expanded` |
| controlled panel | `aria-controls` |
| selected option | `aria-selected` on each option |
| disabled option | `aria-disabled` on each option |

apiSections.title: DropdownComponent - ui-dropdown
apiSections.lead: Standalone dropdown field built on the shared FieldComponent. It supports single and multi-select flows, projected item templates, compact filter mode, and async option loading while integrating with Angular forms through ControlValueAccessor.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Static option list used for local filtering and as the base item set when no async data source is supplied.
Optional async source used for paged loading and server-side search.
Controls whether the component stores one value or a selectable set with tags and checkboxes.
Adds a search field at the top of the panel.
Shows a clear action when a selection exists and the field is editable.
Maximum panel height before the internal scroll container takes over.
Renders the trigger as a compact button instead of the full field shell.
Default icon for compact mode when no selected item icon overrides it.
Page size used by the internal scroll container for static and async loading.
Debounce applied before forwarding search text to the async data source.
Optional lower bound for the overlay panel width when `panelWidth` is not fixed.
Upper bound for the overlay panel width when sizing from the trigger.
Fixed overlay width. When set, it overrides the adaptive min/max sizing behavior.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted when the selected value or selected value array changes.
Emitted after the overlay panel opens.
Emitted after the overlay panel closes.

paragraphs: Projected item content is supported through an ng-template named itemTemplate.
Multi-select uses tags in the trigger and checkboxes in the panel; single-select uses a checkmark in the panel and plain text in the trigger.
The component implements ControlValueAccessor, so it works with ngModel and reactive forms.
blocks.title: Notes
Related mechanisms
items: DropdownHelper.loadSelectedItems() - utility for preloading selected async items when you only start with value ids.
ScrollContainerComponent - powers paging and lazy loading inside the dropdown panel.
FieldComponent - shared base for labels, helper text, error text, validation sync, and aria wiring.
